title: All-role YouTube access, banned/suspended error screens, and YouTube OAuth fix

requirements.summary: Remove role-based access restrictions from all YouTube-related features so users of any role (owner, admin, user, friend) can access them
acceptanceCriteria: A user with role 'user' can see and interact with the ChannelConnection, VideoUrlForm, YouTubePlayer, ClipSuggestions, ClipTimestampControls, and ClipList components
A user with role 'friend' can see and interact with the same YouTube-related components
No YouTube-related UI element is hidden or disabled based on role checks alone

file_operations.description: Remove any conditional rendering or role-based guards around YouTube-related components (VideoUrlForm, YouTubePlayer, ClipTimestampControls, ClipSuggestions, ChannelConnection, ClipList). Ensure all authenticated users see these components regardless of role.
Review and remove any role-based navigation restrictions that might prevent users from accessing YouTube features. Ensure the main app layout does not hide YouTube functionality based on user role.
Remove any role checks that restrict who can connect or disconnect a YouTube channel. All authenticated users should be able to use the connection functionality.
Remove any role-based restrictions on clip actions (delete, download, post to YouTube). Ensure all authenticated users can perform these actions on their clips.
Remove any role-based filtering or display logic that would hide clips from non-admin users. All authenticated users should see the full clip list.
Remove any role checks that restrict who can view or select clip suggestions. All authenticated users should see AI-generated and trending suggestions.
Remove any role-based restrictions on the clip creation form. All authenticated users should be able to set timestamps and create clips.
Remove any role checks that prevent users from entering YouTube URLs or triggering AI clip generation. All authenticated users should be able to load videos.

requirements.summary: Display a full-screen blocking error page for users with 'banned' or 'suspended' account status
acceptanceCriteria: A user whose backend status is 'banned' sees a dedicated error screen explaining their account is banned
A user whose backend status is 'suspended' sees a dedicated error screen explaining their account is suspended
The error screen prevents access to all other app functionality (no navigation around it)
The error screen is shown immediately after login if the status is banned or suspended
Users with active (non-banned, non-suspended) status see the normal app UI

file_operations.description: Create a new component that fetches the current user's profile and checks their status. If status is 'banned' or 'suspended', render a full-screen error page with appropriate messaging and prevent access to children. If status is 'active' or 'inactive', render children normally. Include loading state while fetching profile.

Create a presentational component that displays a full-screen centered error message for banned or suspended accounts. Accept status ('banned' or 'suspended') as a prop and render appropriate messaging, icon, and logout button. Use shadcn/ui Card and Alert components for styling.

Wrap the main app content (HomePage and all routes) inside the AccountStatusGuard component, but place it inside the authentication check. Only authenticated users should have their status checked. The guard must be at the layout level to prevent bypassing via direct navigation.

requirements.summary: Fix YouTube OAuth connection flow to persist channel connection and prevent reversion to disconnected state after callback
acceptanceCriteria: After completing the Google OAuth flow, the ChannelConnection component shows the connected channel info and does not revert to disconnected
The OAuthCallback page waits for the backend actor to be fully ready before attempting to store credentials
On successful credential storage, the useYouTubeChannel and OAuth credential status queries are invalidated and refetched
The backend correctly persists OAuth credentials across calls so isYouTubeConnected returns true after credentials are saved
No race condition exists between actor initialization and credential storage in the OAuth callback

file_operations.description: Enhance the actor readiness check to ensure the backend actor is fully initialized before calling storeGoogleOAuthCredentials. Add explicit polling or retry logic if the actor is not ready. After successful credential storage, invalidate both 'youtubeChannel' and 'hasGoogleOAuthCredentials' query keys to force refetch. Add more detailed error logging for debugging OAuth issues.

Review the initiateGoogleOAuth mutation to ensure it correctly handles the OAuth flow state. After the OAuth callback completes successfully, ensure the isYouTubeChannelConnected query is invalidated and refetched. Add dependency on the actor being ready before enabling queries.

Review the storeGoogleOAuthCredentials mutation and ensure it invalidates all relevant query keys on success: 'youtubeChannel', 'hasGoogleOAuthCredentials', and 'currentUserProfile'. Add error handling for race conditions or actor initialization failures. Ensure mutations wait for actor readiness before executing.

Ensure the component correctly reflects connection state by using both isYouTubeChannelConnected and hasGoogleOAuthCredentials queries. Add a refetch mechanism or polling after returning from OAuth callback to immediately show connected state. Display loading state during refetch to prevent UI flicker.
